title: grevillea acanthifolia subsp. stenomera
intro:
Grevillea acanthifolia subsp. stenomera, also known as the Toothbrush or Honeysuckle Bush, is a shrub species native to southern Australia, characterized by its bright yellow or pink flowers resembling a toothbrush.

The leaves and flowers of this plant have been used in traditional Aboriginal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.

The health benefits of Grevillea acanthifolia subsp. stenomera include its potential to treat various ailments such as colds, coughs, sore throats, and skin irritations due to its antiseptic properties, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce fever.

The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures made from the leaves and flowers, which can be used to treat various health issues, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
